Perpignan pĕrpēnyäN [key], city (1990 pop.
108,049), capital Pyrénées-Orientales dept., France, near Spanish border Mediterranean.
farm trade center, handling wine, fruits, vegetables.
distilleries, canneries, factories making chocolate, clothing, paper, toys.
Tourism also important; there nearby international airport, city thoroughfare motorists going Spain.
Founded c.10th cent., Perpignan fortified capital Spanish kingdom Roussillon
architecture shows much Spanish influence.
Among notable buildings Loge (14th cent.
built house merchants' exchange; Gothic Cathedral Jean (14th-15th cent.
castle kings Majorca (13th-15th cent.
which forms part citadel dominating city.
